content: What is 2+2?
What is 2+2?
What is the agentic loop?

The **agentic loop** is the fundamental cycle that autonomous AI agents follow to accomplish tasks. It typically consists of these stages:

1. **Perceive** — Gather information from the environment (read files, check APIs, observe state)
2. **Think/Reason** — Process information, plan, and make decisions
3. **Act** — Execute actions (write files, call tools, send messages)
4. **Learn/Adapt** — Update based on feedback and results, then repeat

This loop enables agents to work autonomously toward goals, adjusting their approach based on what they observe. It's the core pattern behind systems like me—continuously sensing, deciding, and acting until a task is complete.

Some frameworks expand this to **Sense → Plan → Act → Reflect**, but the core idea is the same: a continuous feedback cycle that drives autonomous behavior.
What labs are available in our LMS?
I'll help you find information about available labs in your LMS. Let me explore the workspace to see what's available.

Describe the architecture of the LMS system
Let me explore the LMS system architecture by examining the codebase structure and documentation.

name: read_file
content:
1| # Lab 8 — The Agent is the Interface
2| 
3| [Sync your fork](https://docs.github.com/en/pull-requests/collaborating-with-pull-requests/working-with-forks/syncing-a-fork#syncing-a-fork-branch-from-the-command-line) regularly — the lab gets updated.
4| 
5| ## Product brief
6| 
7| > Your team has been running the LMS backend for weeks. Everyone queries data through the React dashboard or Swagger UI. Your team lead wants a new kind of interface: an AI agent that anyone can talk to in natural language. Nanobot is a lighter version of OpenClaw, and this kind of agent is becoming the new intelligent AI user interface. Instead of clicking through dashboards, users just ask questions — "which lab has the lowest pass rate?", "any errors in the last hour?" — and the agent figures out which API calls to make.
8| >
9| > Set it up from scratch. Wire it into the system. Then extend it with observability tools so it can answer questions about system health too.
10| 
11| > [!IMPORTANT]
12| > Do the whole lab on your **VM**. You can work through a plain SSH shell or through `VS Code` Remote-SSH. When this guide says `localhost`, it means the VM itself or a forwarded port from that VM. Do not install or run `nanobot` on your main machine.
13| 
14| ## What you will learn
15| 
16| By the end of this lab, you should be able to say:
17| 
18| > 1. I can explain what makes an AI agent different from a regular client like a web app or a bot.
19| >    It is not just a self-hosted chat window: it has tools, skills, memory, and can act proactively.
20| > 2. I set up nanobot from scratch — created the project, installed the framework, connected it to the Qwen API, wired it into Docker Compose, and talked to it.
21| > 3. I saw what a bare agent does without tools (hallucinates) vs. with MCP tools (answers correctly) — and I understand why.
22| > 4. I built MCP tools that let the agent query logs and traces, turning observability data into a conversational interface.
23| > 5. I used the agent to investigate a failure, fix a planted bug, and configure it to report system health proactively.
24| 
25| ## Architecture
26| 
27| By the end of the lab, the system looks like this.
28| 
29| In Lab 7 you built one client around your own LLM loop. Here, the agent becomes a shared system layer that multiple clients can talk to — and that layer has reusable tools, memory, and scheduled actions.
30| 
31| ```
32| [Browser]            [Telegram, optional]
33|     \                       /
34|      \                     /
35|       +---- [Nanobot Agent] ---- [LLM]
36|                  |
37|          +-------+-------+
38|          |               |
39|    [LMS Tools]   [Observability Tools]
40|          |               |
41|    [LMS Backend]    [Logs / Traces]
42|          |
43|     [Postgres]
44| ```
45| 
46| ### What you start with
47| 
48| - **LMS app**: the React dashboard, FastAPI backend, and PostgreSQL database.
49| - **Platform services**: Caddy reverse-proxies all traffic, and the Qwen Code API gives your agent access to the LLM.
50| - **Observability stack**: OpenTelemetry Collector, VictoriaLogs, and VictoriaTraces already collect system telemetry.
51| 
52| ### What you add
53| 
54| - **Nanobot agent**: a natural-language interface to the LMS that can reason, call tools, and answer questions.
55| - **Web chat client**: a WebSocket channel plus a Flutter web UI at `/flutter`, protected by `NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY`.
56| - **New agent capabilities**: LMS MCP tools first, then observability MCP tools, then a scheduled health-check job.
57| 
58| ## Tasks
59| 
60| ### Prerequisites
61| 
62| 1. Complete the [lab setup](./lab/setup/setup-simple.md#lab-setup)
63| 
64| > **Note**: First time in this course? Do the [full setup](./lab/setup/setup-full.md#lab-setup) instead.
65| 
66| ### Required
67| 
68| 1. [Set Up the Agent](./lab/tasks/required/task-1.md) — install nanobot, configure Qwen API, add MCP tools, write skill prompt
69| 2. [Deploy and Connect a Web Client](./lab/tasks/required/task-2.md) — Dockerize nanobot, add WebSocket channel + Flutter chat UI
70| 3. [Give the Agent New Eyes](./lab/tasks/required/task-3.md) — explore observability data, write log/trace MCP tools
71| 4. [Diagnose a Failure and Make the Agent Proactive](./lab/tasks/required/task-4.md) — investigate a failure, schedule in-chat health checks, fix a planted bug
72| 
73| ### Optional
74| 
75| 1. [Add a Telegram Bot Client](./lab/tasks/optional/task-1.md) — same agent, different interface

(End of file — 75 lines total)

name: read_file
content:
1| services:
2|   backend:
3|     build:
4|       context: ./backend
5|       additional_contexts:
6|         workspace: .
7|       args:
8|         REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}
9|     restart: unless-stopped
10|     networks:
11|       - lms-network
12|     ports:
13|       - ${BACKEND_HOST_ADDRESS:?'BACKEND_HOST_ADDRESS is required'}:${BACKEND_HOST_PORT:?'BACKEND_HOST_PORT is required'}:${BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT}
14|     environment:
15|       - NAME=${BACKEND_NAME:?'BACKEND_NAME is required'}
16|       - DEBUG=${BACKEND_DEBUG:?'BACKEND_DEBUG is required'}
17|       - ADDRESS=${BACKEND_CONTAINER_ADDRESS:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_ADDRESS is required'}
18|       - PORT=${BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
19|       - RELOAD=${BACKEND_RELOAD:?'BACKEND_RELOAD is required'}
20|       - UVICORN_HOST=${BACKEND_CONTAINER_ADDRESS:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_ADDRESS is required'}
21|       - UVICORN_PORT=${BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
22|       - UVICORN_RELOAD=${BACKEND_RELOAD:?'BACKEND_RELOAD is required'}
23|       - CORS_ORIGINS=${BACKEND_CORS_ORIGINS:-[]}
24|       - LMS_API_KEY=${LMS_API_KEY:?'LMS_API_KEY is required'}
25|       - BACKEND_ENABLE_INTERACTIONS=${BACKEND_ENABLE_INTERACTIONS:?'BACKEND_ENABLE_INTERACTIONS is required'}
26|       - BACKEND_ENABLE_LEARNERS=${BACKEND_ENABLE_LEARNERS:?'BACKEND_ENABLE_LEARNERS is required'}
27|       - AUTOCHECKER_API_URL=${AUTOCHECKER_API_URL:?'AUTOCHECKER_API_URL is required'}
28|       - AUTOCHECKER_API_LOGIN=${AUTOCHECKER_API_LOGIN:?'AUTOCHECKER_API_LOGIN is required'}
29|       - AUTOCHECKER_API_PASSWORD=${AUTOCHECKER_API_PASSWORD:?'AUTOCHECKER_API_PASSWORD is required'}
30|       - DB_HOST=${CONST_POSTGRESQL_SERVICE_NAME:?'CONST_POSTGRESQL_SERVICE_NAME is required'}
31|       - DB_PORT=${CONST_POSTGRESQL_DEFAULT_PORT:?'CONST_POSTGRESQL_DEFAULT_PORT is required'}
32|       - DB_NAME=${POSTGRES_DB:?'POSTGRES_DB is required'}
33|       - DB_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:?'POSTGRES_USER is required'}
34|       - DB_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?'POSTGRES_PASSWORD is required'}
35|       - OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=${BACKEND_NAME:?'BACKEND_NAME is required'}
36|       - OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=otlp
37|       - OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=none
38|       - OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp
39|       - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:${CONST_OTEL_COLLECTOR_GRPC_PORT:?'CONST_OTEL_COLLECTOR_GRPC_PORT is required'}
40|       - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc
41|       - OTEL_PYTHON_LOG_CORRELATION=true
42|     depends_on:
43|       postgres:
44|         condition: service_healthy
45|       otel-collector:
46|         condition: service_started
47| 
48|   postgres:
49|     image: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}postgres:18.3-alpine
50|     restart: unless-stopped
51|     networks:
52|       - lms-network
53|     environment:
54|       - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:?'POSTGRES_DB is required'}
55|       - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:?'POSTGRES_USER is required'}
56|       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?'POSTGRES_PASSWORD is required'}
57|     ports:
58|       - ${POSTGRES_HOST_ADDRESS:?'POSTGRES_HOST_ADDRESS is required'}:${POSTGRES_HOST_PORT:?'POSTGRES_HOST_PORT is required'}:${CONST_POSTGRESQL_DEFAULT_PORT:?'CONST_POSTGRESQL_DEFAULT_PORT is required'}
59|     volumes:
60|       - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
61|       - ./backend/app/data/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
62|     healthcheck:
63|       test:
64|         [
65|           "CMD-SHELL",
66|           "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:?'POSTGRES_USER is required'} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:?'POSTGRES_DB is required'}",
67|         ]
68|       interval: 5s
69|       timeout: 5s
70|       retries: 5
71| 
72|   pgadmin:
73|     image: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}dpage/pgadmin4:latest
74|     restart: unless-stopped
75|     networks:
76|       - lms-network
77|     environment:
78|       - PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL=${PGADMIN_EMAIL:?'PGADMIN_EMAIL is required'}
79|       - PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD=${PGADMIN_PASSWORD:?'PGADMIN_PASSWORD is required'}
80|       - SCRIPT_NAME=/utils/pgadmin
81|     ports:
82|       - ${PGADMIN_HOST_ADDRESS:?'PGADMIN_HOST_ADDRESS is required'}:${PGADMIN_HOST_PORT:?'PGADMIN_HOST_PORT is required'}:80
83|     depends_on:
84|       postgres:
85|         condition: service_healthy

92|     volumes:

109|   #     - LLM_API_BASE_URL=http://qwen-code-api:${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT:?'QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}/v1
110|   #     - LLM_API_MODEL=${LLM_API_MODEL:?'LLM_API_MODEL is required'}
111|   #     - NANOBOT_LMS_BACKEND_URL=http://backend:${BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
112|   #     - NANOBOT_LMS_API_KEY=${LMS_API_KEY:?'LMS_API_KEY is required'}
113|   #     - NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_ADDRESS=${NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_ADDRESS:?'NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_ADDRESS is required'}
114|   #     - NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_PORT=${NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_PORT:?'NANOBOT_GATEWAY_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
115|   #     - NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_ADDRESS=${NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_ADDRESS:?'NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_ADDRESS is required'}
116|   #     - NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT=${NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT:?'NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
117|   #     - NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY=${NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY:?'NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY is required'}
118|   #   depends_on:
119|   #     - backend
120|   #     - qwen-code-api
121| 
122|   # Task 2B — uncomment after you add nanobot-websocket-channel and want the Flutter build.
123|   # client-web-flutter:
124|   #   build:
125|   #     context: ./nanobot-websocket-channel/client-web-flutter
126|   #     args:
127|   #       REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}
128|   #       REGISTRY_PREFIX_GHCR: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_GHCR:-}
129|   #   volumes:
130|   #     - client-web-flutter:/output
131| 
132|   caddy:
133|     image: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}caddy:2.11-alpine
134|     networks:
135|       - lms-network
136|     depends_on:
137|       - backend
138|       - client-web-react
139|       # Task 2B — uncomment after you enable the Flutter build service above.
140|       # - client-web-flutter
141|       # Task 2A — uncomment after you enable the nanobot service above.
142|       # - nanobot
143|       - qwen-code-api
144|       - victorialogs
145|       - victoriatraces
146|     environment:
147|       - CADDY_CONTAINER_PORT=${CADDY_CONTAINER_PORT:?'CADDY_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
148|       - BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT=${BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT:?'BACKEND_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
149|       # Task 2A — uncomment when you proxy /ws/chat to nanobot.
150|       # - NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT=${NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT:?'NANOBOT_WEBCHAT_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
151|       - QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT=${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT:?'QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
152|       - CONST_PGADMIN_CONTAINER_PORT=${CONST_PGADMIN_CONTAINER_PORT:?'CONST_PGADMIN_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
153|       - CONST_VICTORIALOGS_CONTAINER_PORT=${CONST_VICTORIALOGS_CONTAINER_PORT:?'CONST_VICTORIALOGS_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
154|       - CONST_VICTORIATRACES_CONTAINER_PORT=${CONST_VICTORIATRACES_CONTAINER_PORT:?'CONST_VICTORIATRACES_CONTAINER_PORT is required'}
155|     ports:
156|       - ${GATEWAY_HOST_ADDRESS:?'GATEWAY_HOST_ADDRESS is required'}:${GATEWAY_HOST_PORT:?'GATEWAY_HOST_PORT is required'}:${CADDY_CONTAINER_PORT}
157|     volumes:
158|       - ./caddy/Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile
159|       - client-web-react:/srv/react:ro
160|       # Task 2B — uncomment when you want Caddy to serve the Flutter build output.
161|       # - client-web-flutter:/srv/flutter:ro
162| 
163|   qwen-code-api:
164|     build:
165|       context: ./qwen-code-api
166|       args:
167|         REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}
168|     restart: unless-stopped
169|     networks:
170|       - lms-network
171|     ports:
172|       - ${QWEN_CODE_API_HOST_ADDRESS:?'QWEN_CODE_API_HOST_ADDRESS is required'}:${QWEN_CODE_API_HOST_PORT:?'QWEN_CODE_API_HOST_PORT is required'}:${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT:-8080}
173|     volumes:
174|       - ~/.qwen:/mnt/qwen-creds:ro
175|     environment:
176|       - PORT=${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT:-8080}
177|       - ADDRESS=${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_ADDRESS:-0.0.0.0}
178|       - DEFAULT_MODEL=${QWEN_CODE_API_MODEL:?'QWEN_CODE_API_MODEL is required'}
179|       - LOG_LEVEL=${QWEN_CODE_API_LOG_LEVEL:?'QWEN_CODE_API_LOG_LEVEL is required'}
180|       - MAX_RETRIES=${QWEN_CODE_API_MAX_RETRIES:?'QWEN_CODE_API_MAX_RETRIES is required'}
181|       - RETRY_DELAY_MS=${QWEN_CODE_API_RETRY_DELAY_MS:?'QWEN_CODE_API_RETRY_DELAY_MS is required'}
182|       - QWEN_CODE_AUTH_USE=${QWEN_CODE_API_AUTH_USE:?'QWEN_CODE_API_AUTH_USE is required'}
183|       - LOG_REQUESTS=${QWEN_CODE_API_LOG_REQUESTS:?'QWEN_CODE_API_LOG_REQUESTS is required'}
184|       - QWEN_CODE_API_KEY=${QWEN_CODE_API_KEY:-}
185|     healthcheck:
186|       test:
187|         - CMD
188|         - python
189|         - "-c"
190|         - |
191|           import urllib.request
192|           req = urllib.request.Request(
193|               'http://localhost:${QWEN_CODE_API_CONTAINER_PORT:-8080}/health',
194|               headers={'X-API-Key': '${QWEN_CODE_API_KEY:-}'}
195|           )
196|           urllib.request.urlopen(req)
197|       interval: 30s
198|       timeout: 10s
199|       retries: 3
200|       start_period: 10s
201| 
202|   # Optional Task 1 — uncomment after you add the Telegram client repo in Task 2.
203|   # client-telegram-bot:
204|   #   build:
205|   #     context: ./nanobot-websocket-channel/client-telegram-bot
206|   #     args:
207|   #       REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}
208|   #   restart: unless-stopped
209|   #   networks:
210|   #     - lms-network
211|   #   environment:
212|   #     - BOT_TOKEN=${BOT_TOKEN:?'BOT_TOKEN is required'}
213|   #     - NANOBOT_WS_URL=${NANOBOT_WS_URL:?'NANOBOT_WS_URL is required'}
214|   #     - NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY=${NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY:?'NANOBOT_ACCESS_KEY is required'}
215|   #   depends_on:
216|   #     - nanobot
217| 
218|   victorialogs:
219|     image: ${REGISTRY_PREFIX_DOCKER_HUB:-}victoriametrics/victoria-logs:latest
220|     restart: unless-stopped
221|     networks:
222|       - lms-network
223|     ports:

225|     volumes:
226|       - victorialogs_data:/victoria-logs-data

233|     restart: unless-stopped
234|     networks:

238|     volumes:

242|       - -retentionPeriod=7d

1| # OpenTelemetry Collector configuration
2| #
3| # Pipelines:
4| #   traces  — receives OTLP traces from instrumented services → VictoriaTraces
5| #   logs    — receives OTLP logs from instrumented services  → VictoriaLogs
6| 
7| receivers:
8|   otlp:
9|     protocols:
10|       grpc:
11|         endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
12|       http:
13|         endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
14| 
15| processors:
16|   batch:
17|     timeout: 5s
18|     send_batch_size: 512
19| 
20| exporters:
21|   debug:
22|     verbosity: detailed
23| 
24|   otlphttp/victorialogs:
25|     logs_endpoint: http://victorialogs:9428/insert/opentelemetry/v1/logs
26|     tls:
27|       insecure: true
28| 
29|   otlphttp/victoriatraces:
30|     traces_endpoint: http://victoriatraces:10428/insert/opentelemetry/v1/traces
31|     tls:
32|       insecure: true
33| 
34| service:
35|   pipelines:
36|     traces:
37|       receivers: [otlp]
38|       processors: [batch]
39|       exporters: [otlphttp/victoriatraces]
40|     logs:
41|       receivers: [otlp]
42|       processors: [batch]
43|       exporters: [otlphttp/victorialogs, debug]

name: read_file
content:
1| """Learning Management Service — FastAPI application."""
2| 
3| import logging
4| import time
5| import traceback
6| from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
7| 
8| from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Request
9| from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
10| from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
11| from starlette.middleware.base import RequestResponseEndpoint
12| from starlette.responses import Response
13| 
14| from app.auth import verify_api_key
15| from app.routers import analytics, interactions, items, learners, pipeline
16| from app.settings import settings
17| 
18| logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19| 
20| 
21| @asynccontextmanager
22| async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
23|     # opentelemetry-instrument already installed the LoggingHandler on the root logger
24|     # (via OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp). We only need to fix uvicorn.access, which has
25|     # propagate=False by default, so its HTTP access lines reach the OTel handler.
26|     logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access").propagate = True
27|     yield
28| 
29| 
30| app = FastAPI(
31|     title=settings.app_name,
32|     debug=settings.debug,
33|     description="A learning management service API.",
34|     version="0.1.0",
35|     lifespan=lifespan,
36| )
37| 
38| 
39| @app.exception_handler(Exception)
40| async def unhandled_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
41|     """Return error details in the response for easier debugging."""
42|     tb = traceback.format_exception(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__)
43|     logger.exception(
44|         "unhandled_exception",
45|         extra={"event": "unhandled_exception", "path": request.url.path},
46|     )
47|     return JSONResponse(
48|         status_code=500,
49|         content={
50|             "detail": str(exc),
51|             "type": type(exc).__name__,
52|             "path": request.url.path,
53|             "traceback": tb[-3:],  # last 3 lines of traceback
54|         },
55|     )
56| 
57| 
58| @app.middleware("http")
59| async def log_requests(request: Request, call_next: RequestResponseEndpoint) -> Response:
60|     logger.info(
61|         "request_started",
62|         extra={"event": "request_started", "method": request.method, "path": request.url.path},
63|     )
64|     t0 = time.perf_counter()
65|     response = await call_next(request)
66|     duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
67|     level = logging.ERROR if response.status_code >= 500 else logging.INFO
68|     logger.log(
69|         level,
70|         "request_completed",
71|         extra={
72|             "event": "request_completed",
73|             "method": request.method,
74|             "path": request.url.path,
75|             "status": response.status_code,
76|             "duration_ms": duration_ms,
77|         },
78|     )
79|     return response
80| 
81| 
82| app.add_middleware(
83|     CORSMiddleware,
84|     allow_origins=settings.cors_origins,
85|     allow_methods=["*"],
86|     allow_headers=["*"],
87| )
88| 
89| app.include_router(
90|     items.router,
91|     prefix="/items",
92|     tags=["items"],
93|     dependencies=[Depends(verify_api_key)],
94| )
95| 
96| if settings.enable_interactions:
97|     app.include_router(
98|         interactions.router,
99|         prefix="/interactions",
100|         tags=["interactions"],
101|         dependencies=[Depends(verify_api_key)],
102|     )
103| 
104| if settings.enable_learners:
105|     app.include_router(
106|         learners.router,
107|         prefix="/learners",
108|         tags=["learners"],
109|         dependencies=[Depends(verify_api_key)],
110|     )
111| 
112| app.include_router(
113|     pipeline.router,
114|     prefix="/pipeline",
115|     tags=["pipeline"],
116|     dependencies=[Depends(verify_api_key)],
117| )
118| 
119| app.include_router(
120|     analytics.router,
121|     prefix="/analytics",
122|     tags=["analytics"],
123|     dependencies=[Depends(verify_api_key)],
124| )

(End of file — 124 lines total)

name: read_file
content:
1| """Stdio MCP server exposing LMS backend operations as typed tools."""
2| 
3| from __future__ import annotations
4| 
5| import asyncio
6| import json
7| import os
8| from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable, Sequence
9| from typing import Any
10| 
11| from mcp.server import Server
12| from mcp.server.stdio import stdio_server
13| from mcp.types import TextContent, Tool
14| from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
15| 
16| from mcp_lms.client import LMSClient
17| 
18| _base_url: str = ""
19| 
20| server = Server("lms")
21| 
22| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23| # Input models
24| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25| 
26| 
27| class _NoArgs(BaseModel):
28|     """Empty input model for tools that only need server-side configuration."""
29| 
30| 
31| class _LabQuery(BaseModel):
32|     lab: str = Field(description="Lab identifier, e.g. 'lab-04'.")
33| 
34| 
35| class _TopLearnersQuery(_LabQuery):
36|     limit: int = Field(
37|         default=5, ge=1, description="Max learners to return (default 5)."
38|     )
39| 
40| 
41| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42| # Helpers
43| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44| 
45| 
46| def _resolve_api_key() -> str:
47|     for name in ("NANOBOT_LMS_API_KEY", "LMS_API_KEY"):
48|         value = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
49|         if value:
50|             return value
51|     raise RuntimeError(
52|         "LMS API key not configured. Set NANOBOT_LMS_API_KEY or LMS_API_KEY."
53|     )
54| 
55| 
56| def _client() -> LMSClient:
57|     if not _base_url:
58|         raise RuntimeError(
59|             "LMS backend URL not configured. Pass it as: python -m mcp_lms <base_url>"
60|         )
61|     return LMSClient(_base_url, _resolve_api_key())
62| 
63| 
64| def _text(data: BaseModel | Sequence[BaseModel]) -> list[TextContent]:
65|     """Serialize a pydantic model (or list of models) to a JSON text block."""
66|     if isinstance(data, BaseModel):
67|         payload = data.model_dump()
68|     else:
69|         payload = [item.model_dump() for item in data]
70|     return [TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))]
71| 
72| 
73| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74| # Tool handlers
75| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76| 
77| 
78| async def _health(_args: _NoArgs) -> list[TextContent]:
79|     return _text(await _client().health_check())
80| 
81| 
82| async def _labs(_args: _NoArgs) -> list[TextContent]:
83|     items = await _client().get_items()
84|     return _text([i for i in items if i.type == "lab"])
85| 
86| 
87| async def _learners(_args: _NoArgs) -> list[TextContent]:
88|     return _text(await _client().get_learners())
89| 
90| 
91| async def _pass_rates(args: _LabQuery) -> list[TextContent]:
92|     return _text(await _client().get_pass_rates(args.lab))
93| 
94| 
95| async def _timeline(args: _LabQuery) -> list[TextContent]:
96|     return _text(await _client().get_timeline(args.lab))
97| 
98| 
99| async def _groups(args: _LabQuery) -> list[TextContent]:
100|     return _text(await _client().get_groups(args.lab))
101| 
102| 
103| async def _top_learners(args: _TopLearnersQuery) -> list[TextContent]:
104|     return _text(await _client().get_top_learners(args.lab, limit=args.limit))
105| 
106| 
107| async def _completion_rate(args: _LabQuery) -> list[TextContent]:
108|     return _text(await _client().get_completion_rate(args.lab))
109| 
110| 
111| async def _sync_pipeline(_args: _NoArgs) -> list[TextContent]:
112|     return _text(await _client().sync_pipeline())
113| 
114| 
115| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116| # Registry: tool name -> (input model, handler, Tool definition)
117| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
118| 
119| _Registry = tuple[type[BaseModel], Callable[..., Awaitable[list[TextContent]]], Tool]
120| 
121| _TOOLS: dict[str, _Registry] = {}
122| 
123| 
124| def _register(
125|     name: str,
126|     description: str,
127|     model: type[BaseModel],
128|     handler: Callable[..., Awaitable[list[TextContent]]],
129| ) -> None:
130|     schema = model.model_json_schema()
131|     # Pydantic puts definitions under $defs; flatten for MCP's JSON Schema expectation.
132|     schema.pop("$defs", None)
133|     schema.pop("title", None)
134|     _TOOLS[name] = (
135|         model,
136|         handler,
137|         Tool(name=name, description=description, inputSchema=schema),
138|     )
139| 
140| 
141| _register(
142|     "lms_health",
143|     "Check if the LMS backend is healthy and report the item count.",
144|     _NoArgs,
145|     _health,
146| )
147| _register("lms_labs", "List all labs available in the LMS.", _NoArgs, _labs)
148| _register(
149|     "lms_learners", "List all learners registered in the LMS.", _NoArgs, _learners
150| )
151| _register(
152|     "lms_pass_rates",
153|     "Get pass rates (avg score and attempt count per task) for a lab.",
154|     _LabQuery,
155|     _pass_rates,
156| )
157| _register(
158|     "lms_timeline",
159|     "Get submission timeline (date + submission count) for a lab.",
160|     _LabQuery,
161|     _timeline,
162| )
163| _register(
164|     "lms_groups",
165|     "Get group performance (avg score + student count per group) for a lab.",
166|     _LabQuery,
167|     _groups,
168| )
169| _register(
170|     "lms_top_learners",
171|     "Get top learners by average score for a lab.",
172|     _TopLearnersQuery,
173|     _top_learners,
174| )
175| _register(
176|     "lms_completion_rate",
177|     "Get completion rate (passed / total) for a lab.",
178|     _LabQuery,
179|     _completion_rate,
180| )
181| _register(
182|     "lms_sync_pipeline",
183|     "Trigger the LMS sync pipeline. May take a moment.",
184|     _NoArgs,
185|     _sync_pipeline,
186| )
187| 
188| 
189| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
190| # MCP handlers
191| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
192| 
193| 
194| @server.list_tools()
195| async def list_tools() -> list[Tool]:
196|     return [entry[2] for entry in _TOOLS.values()]
197| 
198| 
199| @server.call_tool()
200| async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any] | None) -> list[TextContent]:
201|     entry = _TOOLS.get(name)
202|     if entry is None:
203|         return [TextContent(type="text", text=f"Unknown tool: {name}")]
204| 
205|     model_cls, handler, _ = entry
206|     try:
207|         args = model_cls.model_validate(arguments or {})
208|         return await handler(args)
209|     except Exception as exc:
210|         return [TextContent(type="text", text=f"Error: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")]
211| 
212| 
213| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
214| # Entry point
215| # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
216| 
217| 
218| async def main(base_url: str | None = None) -> None:
219|     global _base_url
220|     _base_url = base_url or os.environ.get("NANOBOT_LMS_BACKEND_URL", "")
221|     async with stdio_server() as (read_stream, write_stream):
222|         init_options = server.create_initialization_options()
223|         await server.run(read_stream, write_stream, init_options)
224| 
225| 
226| if __name__ == "__main__":
227|     asyncio.run(main())

(End of file — 227 lines total)

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's the **LMS System Architecture**:

---

## 🏗️ LMS Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           CLIENTS LAYER                                  │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐ │
│  │ React Web   │  │ Flutter Web  │  │ Telegram Bot│  │ Nanobot Agent │ │
│  │ Dashboard   │  │ Chat (/flutter)│ │ (optional) │  │ (AI Interface)│ │
│  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬───────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └───────┬───────┘ │
│         │                │                 │                  │         │
│         └────────────────┴─────────────────┴──────────────────┘         │
│                                   │                                     │
│                          ┌────────▼────────┐                            │
│                          │     Caddy       │                            │
│                          │  (Reverse Proxy)│                            │
│                          └────────┬────────┘                            │
└───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
┌───────────────────────────────────▼─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         APPLICATION LAYER                                │

---

## 📦 Core Components

### 1. **Clients Layer**
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **React Web Dashboard** | Primary UI for viewing labs, scores, analytics |
| **Flutter Web Chat** | WebSocket-based chat interface for Nanobot (`/flutter`) |
| **Telegram Bot** (optional) | Alternative chat interface via Telegram |
| **Nanobot Agent** | AI-powered natural language interface with MCP tools |

### 2. **Gateway Layer**
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Caddy** | Reverse proxy routing traffic to all services, serves static builds |

### 3. **Application Layer**
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **LMS Backend (FastAPI)** | REST API with routers: `items`, `learners`, `interactions`, `pipeline`, `analytics` |
| **MCP Server (`mcp_lms`)** | Model Context Protocol server exposing LMS operations as tools for AI agents |
| **Qwen Code API** | LLM gateway providing AI model access to Nanobot |

### 4. **Data Layer**
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **PostgreSQL** | Persistent storage for labs, learners, submissions, scores |
| **PGAdmin** | Database administration UI |

### 5. **Observability Stack**
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **OpenTelemetry Collector** | Receives OTLP traces/logs from instrumented services |
| **VictoriaLogs** | Log storage and query engine |
| **VictoriaTraces** | Distributed traces storage |

---

## 🔧 MCP Tools (Agent Skills)

The `mcp_lms` server provides these tools for the Nanobot agent:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `lms_health` | Check LMS backend health |
| `lms_labs` | List all available labs |
| `lms_learners` | List all registered learners |
| `lms_pass_rates` | Get avg score & attempts per task for a lab |
| `lms_timeline` | Get submission timeline by date |
| `lms_groups` | Get group performance metrics |
| `lms_top_learners` | Get top learners by average score |
| `lms_completion_rate` | Get pass/total ratio for a lab |
| `lms_sync_pipeline` | Trigger data sync pipeline |

---

## 🔄 Data Flow

1. **User Query** → Client (React/Flutter/Telegram/Nanobot)
2. **Request** → Caddy (reverse proxy)
3. **Routing** → LMS Backend (FastAPI) or Nanobot Agent
4. **Agent Reasoning** → Nanobot uses MCP tools → LMS Backend
5. **Data Access** → PostgreSQL
6. **Telemetry** → OTel Collector → VictoriaLogs/VictoriaTraces
7. **Response** → Client

---

## 🎯 Key Design Principles

- **Agent-first**: Nanobot provides a natural language interface to all LMS operations
- **Observability-native**: All services emit OTLP traces/logs for debugging
- **Modular**: MCP tools allow extending agent capabilities without changing core logic
- **Containerized**: All services run in Docker Compose with service-to-service networking
